title: Real Estate Buyer Agent Consultation: Secrets to Win in 2025
excerpt: Navigating the new rules of real estate can feel like a fresh start rather than a hurdle. When the NAR rules kick in on August…
content:
Navigating the new rules of real estate can feel like a fresh start rather than a hurdle. When the NAR rules kick in on August 17, 2024, transparency becomes the name of the game. Buyers will now sign contracts that clearly outline their agent’s fees – no more hidden costs rolled into the MLS listings.

This means, as a buyer agent, it’s critical to communicate these changes, justify your value, and explain the buyer agent contract. But, no worries! You’ll handle it all during your buyer agent presentation and appointment with your prospective client.

Hey, this isn’t really new territory. Listing agents have always had to justify their value and secure contracts. Now, buyer agents must step up and do the same.

Whether you’re an experienced agent or a newbie, we’ve got your back with this comprehensive look at buyer agent presentations. You’ve got this! Here’s your handy dandy index:

  • New NAR Rule Explained
  • Winning Buyer Agent Presentations
  • Buyer Agent Appointment Prep
  • Buyer Agent Appointment – Show Time
  • Post Buyer Agent Appointment
  • Compensation Choices
  • That’s a Wrap
  • Join our FREE Team at eXp Realty
  • FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

New NAR Rule Explained

On August 17, 2024, the National Association of Realtors (NAR) is implementing its big change for buyer agents. From then on, before you can tour homes with clients, you’ll need a written agreement in place that clearly spells out your agency and fees. Yikes! That’s something most buyer agents haven’t done in a very long time.

No worries, the task doesn’t have to be daunting! As a buyer agent, it’s merely time to fine-tune your approach. All you’ll need to do is be clear and upfront with clients. Think detailed buyer presentations that lay it all out before the house hunting even starts.

Winning Buyer Agent Presentations

It’s time to elevate your buyer agent presentation. Forget about those days when a few general bullet points were enough to win over new clients. Now, with clients needing to sign an agreement to pay you even if the home seller doesn’t, they’ll want detailed information about your services.

Showcase the hard work you do behind the scenes. Educate your buyer clients on the value you bring and the effort you put in that often goes unnoticed. It’s your chance to highlight your expertise and dedication, ensuring they understand exactly what they’re getting when they choose you as their agent.

Buyer Agent Presentation Components

In general, all buyer presentations will include you:

  • Introducing Yourself & Your Unique Value Proposition
  • Introducing Your Brokerage & the Home Buying Process

Besides doing those, in these changing times, also plan to add sections about:

  • How Agents Get Paid
  • Pledges and Requests
  • Buyer Contracts

Let’s look at how you can make each of these most compelling.

Agent Introduction

Let’s kick things off by introducing yourself with a burst of enthusiasm! Start with something like: “Hi there, I’m Karrie Hill and I’m absolutely passionate about real estate!”

Next, share a bit about your background to build a connection. For example, “I got into real estate after a rewarding career in law because I realized my true calling was helping people find their dream homes. There’s nothing like the happiness on a client’s face when they find ‘the one.'”

Add a personal touch by mentioning your hobbies or community involvement. For instance, “When I’m not busy showing homes, you’ll find me hiking the beautiful Marin County trails with my two little dogs, Baxter and Lily, or volunteering at the local animal shelter. I love giving back to the community and connecting with nature.”

These personal details make you relatable and showcase your dedication to both your clients and your community. It’s all about creating that warm, trustworthy first impression!

Your Unique Value Proposition

Next, let’s talk about what sets you apart from the competition. Your unique value proposition (UVP) is all about defining and showcasing what makes you the go-to agent for buyers. It’s about highlighting the specific benefits and services you offer that directly address your clients’ needs and pain points.

Start by clearly articulating what you do for your clients, how it benefits them, and why they should choose you over others. For example, you might highlight your extensive experience, deep market knowledge, legal & construction background, savvy use of technology, or innovative approaches to finding the perfect home.

Here are some key elements to include:

Certifications

Highlight any professional certifications you hold. Like being a Certified Residential Specialist (CRS) or Accredited Buyer’s Representative (ABR). These certifications boost your credibility and reassure clients of your expertise. Don’t have any? No worries. Go to the National Association of Realtors and take some certified courses online.

Experience

Leverage your experience if you’ve been an agent for years or tap into your team’s or brokerage’s collective expertise if you’re newer. Mention specific achievements or milestones to show your credibility.

For instance, say, “With over 10 years in the industry, I’ve helped hundreds of families find their dream homes.” Or highlight team success with, “Our team sold $3.5 billion in real estate last year.”

You can also emphasize your brokerage’s reach like “eXp Realty helps more families buy and sell homes than any other independent brokerage.”

Agent Metrics

This is where you can really shine! Do the math and find impressive metrics to show your value. Use your team or brokerage stats if you don’t have personal numbers yet. Here are some key metrics to highlight:

  • Savings: “On average, I save my clients $5,000 through smart negotiations and concessions.”
  • Efficiency: “Most of my clients find their home after touring just five properties.”
  • Speed: “My average client finds a home and closes the deal within 45 days.”
  • Success Rate: “I have an 85% offer acceptance rate, ensuring you get the home you want.”
  • Brokerage or Team Success: “Our brokerage has a track record of selling homes 20% faster than the market average.”

Testimonials

Include glowing testimonials from satisfied clients. For example, “John and Jane Doe said, ‘Working with Karrie Hill was a game-changer. She found us the perfect home and saved us thousands!'”

Getting these fantastic testimonials is easier than you think. Ask for them when your clients are happiest, whether that’s after seeing homes, finding the perfect one, or getting into contract. Don’t wait until after closing, as clients are often busy with their move and less engaged. Make it simple for them by sending a link to leave a Google review. This way, you capture their positive feedback when it’s fresh and enthusiastic.

Brokerage Introduction

Now that you’ve covered why clients should work with you, add a bit of brokerage magic to your presentation. Introduce your brokerage and explain why you chose to work with them. Highlight special tech, support, tools, or access to off-market properties that your brokerage brings to the table.

For example, you might say, “I chose to work with eXp Realty because of their cutting-edge technology and unparalleled support. Our brokerage offers exclusive tools that help me find the best deals, including access to off-market properties that other agents can’t provide. Plus, our team is backed by a network of experienced professionals ready to assist 24/7 with every step of the home-buying process.”

The Home Buying Process

Alright, this is the meat of traditional buyer agent appointments. Typically, agents talk about steps to home buying, like:

  • Set a Budget
  • Search for Homes
  • Tour Homes
  • Make an Offer
  • Negotiate and Contract
  • Work During Escrow
  • Closing Day

But let’s up your game! For each step, don’t miss the chance to explain what you do behind the scenes. Your clients don’t know all the work you put in, and now with contracts that clearly state your pay, you need to explain what you do to earn it.

Reasonable people don’t mind paying for value when they understand it. So, expand each traditional step with a list of your behind-the-scenes work.

Are you not sure what you do at each step? Write it down during your next transaction, or use guides like NAR’s “179 Ways Agents Who Are Realtors Are Worth Every Penny of Their Compensation” and “105 More Ways” to detail your efforts. Make sure to explain terms and what clients should expect at each stage.

Don’t forget to highlight key steps that buyers care about most. For example, explain how you strategize and negotiate deals. Clients want agents who can get them the house they want at the best price.

Show clients every step you take to make their home-buying experience seamless and valuable. They’ll appreciate the transparency and the effort you put into earning your commission.

How Agents Get Paid

Alright, now that your clients will be paying you directly, it’s super important they get the real scoop on how you’re compensated. You know, that big commission check? Most folks don’t realize it’s not all going into your pocket.

Let’s clear that up before anyone jumps to the conclusion you’re taking home the whole pie. Transparency here will help your clients appreciate the hustle behind your service. They’ll understand why every dollar counts! Here’s a simple way to explain it:

Contingency-Based Income

Think of agent pay like lawyers who work on contingency and only get paid when they win your case. Typically agents also only get paid if a deal closes. This means you work for free and no earnings are guaranteed. It’s all or nothing. It’s a high-risk, high-reward setup where your payoff compensates for the many hours you may invest without a return.

Commission Split

The commission you earn on closing a deal isn’t all yours to keep. A portion of it goes to your brokerage, referral partners, transaction coordinators, and sometimes others too. Your commission check is a team effort!

Out-of-Pocket Expenses

Unlike a typical 9-to-5 job where the company picks up the tab for business expenses, as a real estate agent, you’re running your own show. You pay marketing costs, travel expenses, continuing education costs, real estate association dues, home access key fees, and much more! Your commission isn’t just profit. It also keeps your business afloat.

Buyer Agent Pledge

Let’s jazz up your buyer agent presentation with a splash of commitment and charm! Throw in a “Pledge to Please” where you lay out your golden promises—total honesty, crystal-clear transparency, and steadfast support from start to finish.

Here’s how to make your pledge stand out: Commit to honest property assessments, guarantee confidentiality, and always put your client’s interests first. Ensure your clients that you will communicate clearly and promptly, disclose any potential conflicts of interest, show all properties that fit your client’s needs, and thoroughly explain all documents before they sign.

This pledge sets clear expectations and showcases your dedication to ethical practices and client satisfaction. It can strengthen your relationships with clients and help you stand out in a crowded market. Staying true to this pledge will build your reputation as a reliable and trustworthy agent, potentially earning you more referrals and repeat business.

Requests of Clients

Want to keep those home tours smooth and professional? Giving your clients a heads-up on these tips makes the process more efficient and cements your reputation as a pro who’s all about respect and transparency. Share these tips with your clients:

Punctuality is Key

Arriving on time keeps the day running smoothly and shows respect for everyone’s busy schedules! Angering a seller by being late won’t help you win the home.

Chatting During Tours

Discussing offers or sensitive details in the house? Not a great idea. You never know if sellers or other buyers are listening in. Keeping negotiations under wraps until you’re in a private setting protects your strategy and maintains confidentiality.

Buyer Contracts

Alright, let’s dive into the heart of the matter: those essential buyer contracts. From August 17th, thanks to the new N.A.R. rules, you’ll need to have a signed buyer agent agreement in place before you show any homes.

But, it’s all good! Navigating contracts with clarity and flexibility demonstrates your professionalism and builds trust and loyalty with your clients. Here’s how you can handle it with flair:

Introducing the Contract

After rocking your buyer presentation, it’s time to chat about the nitty-gritty—signing that contract. This isn’t just paperwork; it’s the blueprint of your relationship with your client. It outlines both parties’ responsibilities and details your compensation.

Flexibility is Key

Everyone loves options, right? Depending on how well you know your client, you could suggest different types of agreements. For a well-acquainted client, think about proposing an exclusive, long-term contract covering any property. For newer clients, maybe start with a non-exclusive, short-term agreement focused on specific areas. This flexibility helps put clients at ease, giving them control over how they commit.

Cancellation Policy

Let’s talk about making exits as smooth as entries. It’s wise to clarify your cancellation policy upfront. Whether it’s a no-fuss immediate cancellation or a more standard 30-day notice, make sure they know their options. Being clear about cancellation terms prevents future headaches and preserves good vibes. This keeps the door open for referrals down the road.

Clarify the Fine Print

Be upfront about what cancellation means. For instance, if a client decides to back out, are they still on the hook for showings you’ve already done? Spell out these details to avoid misunderstandings and ensure everyone is on the same page.

Buyer Agent Appointment Prep

Now that you’ve written an awesome buyer agent presentation, let’s go over how to prepare for your client appointment.

Mindset Mastery

Prepare yourself to step into every client meeting radiating confidence and positivity. It doesn’t matter if the market’s in a slump or soaring. Your job is to assure buyers that they’re on the right track to finding their dream home.

Maintain a positive and professional demeanor reassures your clients and makes them feel more at ease and confident in their decisions. So pump up your positive energy and let it shine. Your vibe can make all the difference in their home-buying experience.

Market Mastery

Brush up on your market mastery. A solid grasp of the current real estate market is key so get ready to quote those stats. Before meeting with clients, update yourself on the latest prices, inventory levels, and market trends.

Demonstrating your knowledge instills confidence in your clients and positions you as an expert they can trust. This prep ensures you’re ready to answer questions with authority and insight.

Buyer Agent Presentation Mastery

Last, review your Buyer Agent Presentation and jazz it up to ensure that it sticks with your clients. You’ve invested a heap of effort into gathering this info, so let’s make sure it captivates and sticks with your clients.

Tech Savvy

First up, dial up your presentation engagement with some slick tech. Consider whipping up a dynamic PowerPoint or a snazzy Canva presentation. Using animations, vibrant photos and creative designs keeps your clients engaged.

Takeaways

Make sure your clients walk away with something tangible. Whether it’s printing out the key points or emailing them the entire presentation, giving them a copy ensures they can revisit the info anytime. This helps reinforce what they’ve learned and keeps you top of mind.

Buyer Agent Appointment – Show Time

Alright, you’re all set for your client meeting. But remember, a stellar client meeting isn’t just about running through your presentation. It’s about engaging actively with your clients, weaving seamless conversations into your pitch to really connect.

Here are some key strategies to keep the dialogue serving your interests:

Client-Centric Communication

Focus on listening more than talking. Engage your clients with open-ended questions to better understand their thoughts and concerns. Adapt your responses and your presentation to meet their specific needs.

Understanding Your Client

Get to know what your clients are really looking for. Don’t just focus on the basic requirements like bedrooms or location; dive into their lifestyle needs, future plans, and personal preferences.

Consider using a Google Form to gather the basic information before the meeting. Then you can focus on the deeper information at the appointment. This is your chance to understand beyond the surface details and into the life they envision in their new home.

Tailoring the Conversation

Customize your discussion based on whether they are experienced homebuyers or first-timers. For seasoned buyers, streamline the process information; for newbies, explain each step carefully to build confidence.

Also, tailor your chats to sync with your buyer’s vibes! Celebrate their milestones like buying their first home or moving cities, and pump it all up the enthusiasm. Tap into their emotions that buying a home involves – stress, excitement, fear, whatever. Show them you get it! Your empathy will ease their nerves and make their home-buying experience better.

Incorporate Stories

Share anecdotes from past clients with similar backgrounds or needs. These stories help current buyers visualize their success and connect more deeply with the process. Emphasize the positive impacts and emotional satisfaction of previous clients.

Post Buyer Agent Appointment

You’ve successfully presented your buyer agent presentation, now what?

Follow Up

After the presentation, you’ll want to check in with potential clients. Ask if they have any questions. This shows them that you are committed. You can also send thank you notes or emails. It’s a nice gesture that can set you apart from others. don’t be afraid to ask them to sign the buyer agent agreement so you can start showing them homes.

All Interactions

Once they become your client, always put your best foot forward. When interacting with them, review their client file to be up on their particular facts and needs. While out looking at homes also focus on educating your buyers, not pushing them to a purchase. Providing them with knowledge empowers buyers and makes them feel valued and respected, keeping the dynamic not a sales transaction but rather an advisory relationship.

Compensation Choices

With all the prior information, you are ready to rock your buyer agent role. However, let’s cover one more thing that might be nagging at you.

That is, now the new N.A.R. rules say your compensation will no longer be a percentage of the sales price offered through the MLS. Without that constraint, buyer agents are likely to make more creative choices about their compensation.

Let’s go over some of those. Of course, all the following ideas are subject to both the rules of your state and your brokerage. So, check on those.

Percentage-Based Commission

You already know this one. This traditional model involves charging a percentage of the home’s sale price, typically between 2.5% to 3% for the buyer’s agent. Let your buyers know that this is what you are paid when a deal closes.

Stick with this percentage-based commission structure if you plan to provide a full suite of services listed in your buyer agent presentation.

Flat Fee Structure

Ideal for high-value property markets, the flat fee model lets buyers sidestep the heftier percentage-based commissions. For a fixed amount – say, $9,999 – buyers can enjoy significant savings, especially on more expensive properties.

You’ll need to clearly define which services you’ll provide in exchange for a flat fee.

Pay Per Service

This a la carte approach allows clients to pay only for the services they use, offering flexibility and potentially lowering costs. It’s crucial to outline what each service entails and ensure the pricing is transparent and fair.

Non-Refundable Retainer Fee

A non-refundable retainer fee can help agents sort out the real buyers from the unmotivated buyers by requiring that they have some skin in the game. This fee secures your services while ensuring you will be paid something for your time even if the client never closes a deal.

The Caveat

Remember, under the new rules, you set the price ceiling on what you can earn.

That means that even if a seller offers a buyer agent more than what you contracted for, you will not get more than your contract amount. This means that if a seller throws in a sweetener that exceeds your agreed-upon fee, you’re still capped at your contract rate – you can’t get more.

However, you’ve still got the freedom to slide those numbers down whenever it suits the situation. Why might you drop your fee? Maybe you wrapped up a deal faster than a New York minute, or perhaps shaving off a few percentage points could rescue a nearly dead deal. Or, you might decide to match a lower offer from a seller just to keep things smooth.

You may want to set your fee high and adjust as needed, giving you the flexibility to adapt to different scenarios. It’s all about making your commission work for you and your client, ensuring everyone walks away happy.

That’s a Wrap

Navigating the buyer agent appointment process just got easier. You’ve learned the ropes from understanding the new NAR rule to nailing your buyer agent presentation.

Would you like an animated, beautiful buyer agent presentation that is already complete? One that you can easily customize to your information and needs?  Join our free eXp Realty team and Smart Agent Alliance has you covered.

title: eXp Revenue Share 2.0: What Changed and How Earnings Are Unlocked Faster
excerpt: Ready to earn more income easier and faster? Learn about eXp Revenue Share 2.0 and how it boosts agents’ income!
content:
Key Takeaway: eXp Revenue Share 2.0 is a set of program updates that changed how quickly agents can qualify for and access revenue share, without changing the underlying seven-tier structure or the company dollar funding source. The updates reduced certain qualification thresholds, introduced Fast Start bonus rules, and added an optional “Pay Now” payout feature, all governed by defined eligibility requirements.

TL;DR About eXp Revenue Share 2.0

  • Revenue Share 2.0 adjusted qualification rules, not the seven-tier structure
  • Revenue share is paid from eXp’s company dollar
  • Tier 1 includes directly sponsored agents, expanding through Tier 7
  • Only unlocked tiers generate revenue share payouts
  • Certain FLQA qualification requirements were adjusted under Revenue Share 2.0
  • “Pay Now” is an optional feature that changes payout timing
  • Retirement or estate payments require continued license affiliation
  • Fast Start bonuses apply to qualifying first-year sponsorship production

Revenue Share 2.0 refers to a set of updates eXp Realty made to the existing revenue share program. These updates adjusted certain qualification thresholds, first-year bonus rules, and payout timing options, while leaving the underlying seven-tier structure and company dollar funding model unchanged.

This article explains how eXp Revenue Share 2.0 fits into the broader eXp Realty income ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your index:

Table of Contents

Revenue Share 2.0 Program Changes
Revenue Share Funding and Tier Structure
Sponsorship Roles and Network Behavior
Revenue Share in Context of Other Brokerage Models and Income Streams
What Agents Also Ask About eXp Revenue Share 2.0 Changes
Why This Topic Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
What are Fast Start bonuses in Revenue Share 2.0?
What is the “Pay Now” feature and what does it change?
Can revenue share be passed to heirs under eXp’s program?
Does revenue share replace traditional retirement plans for some agents?
Related eXp Realty Income Topics

Revenue Share 2.0 Program Changes

Revenue Share 2.0 introduced specific changes to how agents qualify for revenue share and how certain payments are accessed. These updates did not replace the underlying seven-tier structure or funding source but adjusted qualification thresholds, first-year incentives, and payout timing options.

Revenue Share 2.0 Reduced Certain FLQA Barriers for Early Tier Earnings

A central change under Revenue Share 2.0 is the removal of FLQA requirements for Tiers 2 and 3. Under prior versions, agents were required to sponsor a defined number of First Line Qualifying Agents (FLQAs) before earning revenue share from those tiers. Revenue Share 2.0 removed those thresholds for early tiers, allowing revenue share to be paid from Tiers 2 and 3 without first meeting FLQA counts, subject to current program rules.

This change alters how revenue share eligibility from early tiers is structured when existing agents are placed within a sponsorship network. The underlying sponsorship and production requirements continue to apply under current program rules.

Example (Illustrative Only):

Agents have always had full discretion over how sponsored agents are placed within Tier 1 and Tier 2. That discretion did not change under Revenue Share 2.0.

What changed is how Tier 2 revenue share eligibility is triggered. Previously, Tier 2 revenue share required five Frontline Qualifying Agents (FLQAs) in Tier 1. Under Revenue Share 2.0, Tier 2 revenue share is available immediately without a Tier 1 FLQA requirement.

Because Tier 1 placement is commonly associated with more direct support responsibility, removing the Tier 1 threshold allows tier placement decisions to be made independently of Tier 2 eligibility, without altering sponsorship or production requirements.

Revenue Share 2.0 Added Fast Start Bonus Rules for First-Year Sponsorship Earnings

Under Revenue Share 2.0, sponsors continue to earn standard revenue share from Tier 1 agents based on production. During a Tier 1 agent’s first year at eXp Realty, a Fast Start bonus applies, allowing the sponsor to earn up to $4,000 from that agent under a defined first-year payout structure.

This first-year payout structure applies only during the sponsored agent’s initial year. After that period, revenue share from the agent follows the standard ongoing payout structure, which provides a minimum of $1,400, plus any applicable adjustment bonus, and remains subject to normal production and program rules.

Revenue Share 2.0 Added an Optional “Pay Now” Payout Timing Feature

Revenue share payouts are issued on a monthly schedule, typically during the third week of the following month after transactions closed. Revenue Share 2.0 introduced an optional “Pay Now” feature that allows agents to receive revenue share funds earlier, closer to the time a transaction closes. This feature does not change how revenue share is calculated or earned. It only affects when funds are made available and is subject to applicable program terms and fees.

Revenue Share Funding and Tier Structure

Revenue share at eXp Realty follows defined rules governing where funds originate and how sponsorship tiers determine payout eligibility. These mechanics apply regardless of agent role or production level.

Revenue Share Is Funded from the Company Dollar and Tied to the Cap

eXp Realty agents generally operate on an 80/20 commission split until they reach the annual company cap of $16,000, after which they earn 100% of their commissions for the remainder of the capping year.

The 20% portion retained by eXp before an agent caps is referred to as the company dollar. eXp allocates 50% of the company dollar to the revenue share pool, which is distributed to eligible agents through the seven-tier sponsorship structure. The remaining 50% of the company dollar is retained by eXp Realty to fund operating expenses and company profits.

The Seven-Tier Structure Still Controls Where Revenue Share Can Be Generated

Revenue share continues to operate across seven tiers of sponsorship. Tier 1 consists of agents you personally sponsor when they join eXp Realty. Tier 2 consists of agents sponsored by your Tier 1 agents. This pattern continues through Tier 7 as additional sponsorship layers form. Revenue share is only paid from tiers that are unlocked and only when agents in those tiers close transactions that generate company dollar.

“Unlocked Tiers” Determine Whether You Are Eligible to Receive Payouts

A tier being “unlocked” means you are eligible to receive revenue share from productive agents within that tier. If a tier is not unlocked, agents may still exist within that tier in your sponsor network, but revenue share is not paid from that tier until the unlock criteria are met. Tiers 1 through 3 are unlocked automatically. Deeper tiers require meeting production-based criteria or qualifying agent thresholds, depending on the program rules in effect.

Sponsorship Roles and Network Behavior

Revenue share outcomes depend on how agents are connected through sponsorship and how production occurs within those relationships. These concepts explain who generates revenue share and under what conditions.

Frontline Agents and FLQAs Define the Earliest Earning Layer and Tier Unlocking

Agents you personally sponsor are commonly referred to as frontline agents (FLAs) or Tier 1 agents. A Tier 1 agent becomes a First Line Qualifying Agent (FLQA) after meeting defined production thresholds, specifically closing at least two transactions or earning $5,000 in gross commission income within a six-month period.

Under eXp Realty’s revenue share rules, the number of FLQAs an agent sponsors is one of the mechanisms used to unlock eligibility for revenue share payouts from deeper tiers. While agents automatically earn on Tier 1 – 3, revenue share from Tiers 4 through 7 is not paid unless the required FLQA thresholds are met or alternative production-based qualification criteria apply.

Exponential Growth Is a Network Effect, Not a Guaranteed Outcome

Sponsor networks can grow exponentially across tiers. For example, if an agent sponsors two producing agents, and each of those agents sponsors two producing agents, the network can expand rapidly across tiers, reaching 128 agents by Tier 7.

This example assumes consistent sponsorship and sustained production and is intended to explain structure rather than predict outcomes. This example is illustrative only and does not predict earnings, production levels, or sponsorship outcomes.

Revenue Share in Context of Other Brokerage Models and Income Streams

Revenue share operates alongside other income structures at eXp Realty and differs from compensation models used by other brokerages. These distinctions affect how payouts are calculated and compared.

Published Revenue Share Charts Emphasize Minimum Payouts Rather Than Maximum Claims

eXp Realty publishes revenue share charts that show minimum payout amounts per capping agent at each tier. These figures represent baseline payments rather than projected or maximum earning scenarios. In practice, additional distributions from bonus pools, particularly within Tiers 1 through 3, have historically increased actual payouts above the published minimums.

eXp Realty’s approach contrasts with compensation models that advertise maximum payout amounts without clearly disclosing how those amounts are reduced by monthly adjustments. By publishing minimum payout amounts instead, eXp establishes a baseline that does not rely on optimistic assumptions and allows agents to understand the lowest possible payout before any positive adjustments are applied.

Revenue Share Is One Component of Multiple eXp Income Streams

Revenue share is described as one of several potential income streams at eXp Realty. Other commonly referenced income sources include transaction commissions, referral income, and stock awards or purchases tied to eXp’s stock programs.

Each income stream has separate rules, timing, and eligibility requirements. Revenue share operates independently of commission income and does not replace it. For some agents, revenue share may grow large enough to function as a primary income source, even while the agent continues to earn commissions from active production.

Profit Share and Revenue Share Differ in What Funds the Pool

Profit share programs (like at Keller Williams) distribute payouts from a market center’s net profits after operating expenses are deducted. As a result, payouts can vary based on profitability, cost structure, and expense management.

Revenue share programs (like at eXp Realty) distribute payouts from revenue generated by transactions before expenses are applied. Because revenue share is tied to transaction activity rather than net profit, the calculation of the payout pool follows a different funding method based on transaction revenue rather than net profit.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp Revenue Share 2.0 Changes

Is Revenue Share 2.0 a new program or a change to the existing system?

Revenue Share 2.0 is best described as a set of changes to the existing revenue share program rather than a new program. The seven-tier structure and company dollar funding concept remain in place. The changes focus on qualification thresholds, first-year bonus rules, and optional payout timing features, which can affect how quickly agents begin receiving revenue share.

Does Revenue Share 2.0 mean tiers are easier to earn from automatically?

Not entirely. While Revenue Share 2.0 removed FLQA requirements for Tiers 2 and 3, access to Tiers 4 through 7 still requires qualification. Agents may unlock these tiers either by sponsoring a required number of First Line Qualifying Agents (FLQAs) or by meeting annual production benchmarks.

Is Revenue Share 2.0 mainly about higher or faster payouts?

Revenue Share 2.0 introduced changes that affect payout timing options and first-year bonus eligibility under defined conditions. These updates do not change how revenue share is calculated, which tiers exist, or how company dollar is generated.

Can a strong producer benefit from Revenue Share 2.0 without recruiting?

Yes. eXp does not require agents to recruit. Sponsorship can occur through ordinary professional relationships, including when agents ask peers about brokerage experiences and later choose a sponsor. Revenue share still requires sponsored agent production to generate company dollar, and tier unlocking requirements still apply, but active recruiting activity is not required for sponsorship to occur.

Why This Topic Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Revenue Share 2.0 is designed to address earlier-stage qualification friction and payout timing limitations, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

How does eXp Realty revenue share work under the 2.0 updates?

eXp Realty revenue share continues to pay across seven tiers based on sponsorship relationships and transaction production that generates company dollar. Revenue Share 2.0 adjusted certain qualification and payout features, including reduced FLQA requirements for some early tiers, Fast Start bonuses tied to first-year sponsorship production, and an optional “Pay Now” feature that affects payout timing. The underlying tiered, production-driven structure remains unchanged.

What is a Front Line Qualifying Agent (FLQA) and why does it matter?

A Front Line Qualifying Agent (FLQA) is a personally sponsored agent who meets defined production thresholds, such as closing at least two transactions or earning $5,000 in gross commission income within six months. FLQAs are used as one of the qualification methods for unlocking revenue share eligibility from deeper tiers, particularly Tiers 4 through 7, under current program rules.

What are Fast Start bonuses in Revenue Share 2.0?

Fast Start bonuses are first-year sponsorship incentives paid when newly sponsored agents generate qualifying production. During a Tier 1 agent’s first year at eXp Realty, sponsors may earn up to $4,000 from that agent instead of the standard ongoing annual payout amount. After the first year, revenue share from that agent reverts to the normal tier-based payout structure, subject to production.

What is the “Pay Now” feature and what does it change?

“Pay Now” is an optional payout timing feature that allows agents to access earned revenue share funds closer to the time a transaction closes rather than waiting for the standard monthly payout schedule. This feature does not change how revenue share is calculated, which tiers are eligible, or how tiers are unlocked. It only affects when funds are made available, subject to applicable fees and terms.

Can revenue share be passed to heirs under eXp’s program?

Yes. Revenue share may continue to heirs when a qualifying real estate license remains affiliated with eXp Realty and current policy requirements are met. The sponsored agent network must also continue generating transactions that produce company dollar. If licensing affiliation or eligibility requirements are not maintained, revenue share payments stop. Continuation is governed by eXp Realty’s current policies and agreements.

Does revenue share replace traditional retirement plans for some agents?

For some highly productive eXp Realty agents, revenue share functions as a primary retirement income source rather than a supplemental one. This depends on maintaining required licensing affiliation and continued production within the sponsor network. Revenue share payments remain variable, policy-dependent, and tied to transaction activity, and many agents continue earning commission income alongside revenue share during retirement.

  • How Revenue Share Works at eXp Realty
  • eXp Realty Income Streams Explained

title: How eXp Healthcare Coverage Is Structured for Agents
excerpt: Learn how eXp Realty Healthcare helps agents save money and get top-quality care. Discover affordable healthcare options designed specifically for realtors.
content:
Key Takeaway: eXp Healthcare is an optional eXp Realty solution designed for independent agents who want lower healthcare costs and flexible coverage options. It uses curated partnerships and guided plan selection to evaluate healthcare options while operating separately from the core brokerage platform.

TL;DR About eXp Healthcare at eXp Realty

  • eXp Healthcare is optional, not required
  • Built for independent contractors, not W-2 employees
  • Cost structures are often lower than traditional open-market plans
  • Multiple plan paths are evaluated, not one-size-fits-all
  • Enrollment is available year-round
  • Coverage works alongside, not in place of, the brokerage platform

Let’s talk about the elephant in the room, healthcare. Or more accurately, the lack of it. Between managing transactions, lead generation, and running an independent business, many real estate agents are left navigating expensive or inadequate health insurance on their own.

eXp Realty addressed this long-standing gap by partnering with Clearwater Benefits to create an optional healthcare solution designed specifically for agents. Instead of forcing plans or limiting choices, eXp Healthcare focuses on reducing cost, improving coverage options, and providing expert guidance.

This article explains how eXp Healthcare fits into the broader eXp Realty Solutions ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

Clearwater Benefits
Wide Range of Healthcare Options
How eXp Realty Healthcare Is Structured
eXp Realty Healthcare vs Other Brokerages
Success Stories
What Plans Do eXp Agents Typically Choose?
Is it easy to sign up for eXp Realty Healthcare?
What Agents Also Ask About eXp Healthcare
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Solutions Guides

Clearwater Benefits

eXp partnered with Clearwater Benefits to provide U.S.-based eXp agents and their families access to alternative healthcare structures and plan guidance.

Agents commonly describe the following outcomes when reviewing plans through Clearwater guidance:

  • Save substantially on fixed medical costs
  • Save substantially by reducing your out-of-pocket risk
  • Upgrade your network of doctors and hospitals

eXp Realty Healthcare by Clearwater provides a unique balance between affordability and high-quality coverage. Many plans include low copays, low out-of-pocket costs, and zero-dollar deductibles.

Agents get the healthcare they need at prices substantially below traditional open-market health plans.

Wide Range of Healthcare Options

eXp Realty Healthcare offers a wide range of healthcare options. The best part? You don’t have to figure it out yourself. That’s because eXp Realty Healthcare provides a personalized approach.

eXp Realty Healthcare meticulously assesses each agent’s situation to determine the most cost-effective plans without compromising on quality. The experts provide plan comparisons and guidance based on individual circumstances.

Let’s review the three primary ways an eXp agent may go:

eXp Realty Healthcare Solution

Crafted by Clearwater, this is the gold standard for maximizing your benefits while minimizing your costs. Think top-notch network access and minimized risk—all served up with a side of serious savings. We’ll unpack the juicy details shortly.

Traditional Health Insurance

Prefer the tried and true? This option provides traditional marketplace plans. The best part? There are no extra fees to get these through eXp Realty Healthcare. Our team will navigate the maze of plans to find the one that fits you like a glove.

Supplemental Insurance

And because we love options, there’s also supplemental insurance. Dental, vision, accident, critical illness, and disability insurance. Tailor your coverage to fit your life and sleep easy, knowing you’re fully covered.

How eXp Realty Healthcare Is Structured

Agents Save More Dollars Every Year

One commonly cited consideration is reduced fixed healthcare costs. On average, eXp agents bank more than $8,000 each year from switching to eXp Realty Healthcare – that’s a whopping 60% or more reduction in fixed medical expenses. But the savings don’t stop there. Clearwater reports an eye-popping 80% drop in out-of-pocket costs for eXp agents compared to traditional health insurance plans. Imagine keeping more of your hard-earned cash while still enjoying top-notch medical care.

Same Quality Care with Nationwide Open Networks

Switch to eXp Realty Healthcare, and you won’t sacrifice quality for cost. Thanks to access to two of the nation’s largest PPO networks, finding the perfect doctor or specialist is a breeze. Love your current doctor? No worries, you’re likely to keep them thanks to the extensive network options.

No Limits, Maximum Payouts, or Hassles

Say goodbye to the usual limits on doctor visits, labs, and prescriptions. With eXp, the coverage keeps on giving, without caps to hold you back. If you hate dealing with logistics, you’ll love the Care Coordination team. They help you find providers and schedule the care you need for labs, imaging, and hospital services. Plus, when you use this service, your copays and out-of-pocket costs are waived. You heard that right! Your care will be free!

Expert Second Opinion services

That’s right! At eXp Realty Healthcare, you’ll get the added perk of having access to life-saving consultations from leading specialists in their field for that all-important second opinion.

No Enrollment Deadlines

Forget about enrollment deadlines. eXp Realty Healthcare lets you jump on board any time of the year. eXp agents are not inconvenienced by those traditional medical enrollment deadlines.

Mental Healthcare

And there’s more. eXp Realty, through Clearwater, also provides Telemedicine to bring agents high-quality virtual mental health services. eXp Realty is always looking out for the best for their agents.

eXp Realty Healthcare vs Other Brokerages

This chart says it all. When comparing eXp Realty with other big brokerages such as Coldwell Banker, Remas, KW, Berkshire Hathoway, Compass, and even Real, eXp shines. Of all these brokerages, only eXp offers exclusive health plans for their agents. Additionally, only eXp Realty has innovative healthcare plans that save agents thousands of dollars every year.

Success Stories

Ready for some personal actual agent stories? When eXp Realty Healthcare became an option, many eXp agents decided to give it a go. The following examples reflect individual agent experiences and are not representative of all outcomes. Here are some of their stories.

Cancer Diagnosis – $8000 Saved Annually

At 26, Florida realtor Beth Silverman fought breast cancer alongside struggles to secure and afford health insurance as an independent contractor. Beth said, “Being a real estate agent meant endless battles for insurance that normal people who have not been touched by cancer can never understand.”

Now in remission, Beth faced recurring challenges such as annual re-enrollment, soaring premiums, and restricted networks. By switching to eXp Realty Healthcare, Beth saves roughly $8,000 annually on premiums. She says, “I have a cost-sharing plan through eXp, and it’s different than what people are used to. It’s community-based, and trust me … I’m saving thousands in the yearly premium, and prescription costs are a bit cheaper.”

Same Doctors, Same Care Quality – $10,000 Saved Annually

Joe Sinnona from Long Beach, New York, an eXp agent, discovered substantial savings with eXp Realty Healthcare Clearwater compared to his previous health plan. In 2018, Joe was paying $1,500 a month for his family’s health insurance, and the cost was set to increase to $1,850.

Facing these rising costs, Joe and his wife considered dropping their coverage but ultimately found a more affordable solution through eXp. Now, they pay just $791 monthly for the family’s healthcare and have even added dental coverage for an additional $100 a month.

Joe shares, “Same doctors, same quality of care. I raised the deductible and was able to get this affordable healthcare plan through eXp Realty.” He aims to serve as a testament to other agents seeking reliable and cost-effective health insurance.

Same Doctors – $12,000 Saved Annually

Drew Schroeder from Indianapolis, an eXp Realty ICON Agent, found himself reevaluating his health insurance as costs soared to $1,588 per month. After consulting his financial advisors and discussing the eXp Realty Healthcare Clearwater plan via a thorough Zoom meeting, Drew was convinced by the plan’s value.

In Drew’s words, “This is a special program your company is offering you. You need to do it.” By switching, Drew saves over $12,000 annually while maintaining the same doctors for his family. He appreciates how eXp helps bridge common gaps for agents, allowing him to focus on family and his career without the stress of healthcare costs.

Innovative Solutions for the Best Solution

When Gogo Bethke’s husband left his corporate job, the family lost their employer-provided health insurance. Gogo was shocked at the high cost of open-market healthcare. Previously paying around $900 monthly, including coverage for their son who has Type 1 diabetes, they needed an affordable alternative.

Clearwater offered a personalized solution, splitting their son’s coverage into an open market plan and the rest of the family’s into a Clearwater plan. Now, Gogo pays about $400 for her son’s plan and $865 for the family, which is significantly less than potential open-market costs.

Gogo said “It’s still much cheaper than the open market where we would be paying three times the cost”. Plus, Gogo says that the Clearwater plan her family chose offers very inexpensive prescriptions, diagnostic services, and doctor visits.

What Plans Do eXp Agents Typically Choose?

Are you wondering what health plans eXp agents often select after discussing their unique situations with eXp Realty Healthcare experts? Based on internal Clearwater guidance patterns reported to agents:

85% of eXp agents find that Clearwater is a great fit. This includes people who are generally healthy and under 65 years old.

10% of eXp agents get traditional health insurance through advice from Clearwater at no additional cost than the open market provides. This is a good fit for people with pre-existing conditions.

5% of eXp agents are advised by Clearwater to stay on their current plan. But, even if you don’t make a switch, it’s great to have the Clearwater professionals let you know that you are already on the right plan.

So, the vast majority of eXp agents end up with a Clearwater plan. But, all of them get expert advice to determine what is best for them.

Is it easy to sign up for eXp Realty Healthcare?

Absolutely! Choosing the right plan is simple with the guidance of the eXp Realty Healthcare team. Clearwater states that it takes less than five minutes to enroll. Getting healthcare is fast and simple for eXp agents.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp Healthcare

Is eXp Healthcare required if I join eXp Realty?
No. eXp Healthcare is optional and does not replace the brokerage relationship. Agents may choose to use eXp Healthcare, select traditional open-market insurance, or remain on an existing plan. Participation has no impact on commission splits, caps, sponsorship placement, or standing with eXp Realty.

How does eXp Healthcare compare to health benefits at traditional brokerages?
Most real estate brokerages do not offer healthcare options for independent contractors. eXp Healthcare differs by providing access to curated healthcare solutions and expert guidance, allowing agents to evaluate lower-cost and alternative structures rather than navigating open-market plans alone.

Who is eXp Healthcare best suited for?
eXp Healthcare is commonly used by independent agents under 65 who want lower fixed healthcare costs and predictable out-of-pocket exposure. It may not be ideal for every situation, which is why agents are evaluated individually rather than placed into a single plan.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Healthcare is designed to address one of the most common blind spots for independent agents, affordable and functional healthcare, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do all eXp Realty agents receive access to eXp Healthcare?
Yes. U.S.-based eXp Realty agents are eligible to explore eXp Healthcare through Clearwater Benefits. Access is available to all qualifying agents regardless of production level, team affiliation, or sponsor, and agents are free to decide whether participation makes sense for them.

Is enrollment in eXp Healthcare mandatory or automatic?
No. Enrollment is never mandatory and does not occur automatically. Agents must actively choose to engage with eXp Healthcare and review options with Clearwater Benefits. Agents may opt out entirely without any effect on their brokerage relationship.

Are there enrollment periods or deadlines for eXp Healthcare?
No. Unlike traditional health insurance, eXp Healthcare does not require agents to enroll during annual open enrollment periods. Agents may explore options and enroll at any time during the year based on personal or financial changes.

Does eXp Healthcare replace traditional health insurance?
It can, but not always. Depending on an agent’s needs, Clearwater may recommend alternative cost-sharing solutions, traditional marketplace insurance, supplemental coverage, or remaining on an existing plan. eXp Healthcare functions as guided access, not a replacement mandate.

Related eXp Realty Solutions Guides

  • eXp Realty Offices Exist: Here’s How They Work!
  • Success Lending eXp: eXp Realty Agents Provide Mortgage Solutions
  • Curbio + eXp Realty: The Hassle-Free Way to Boost Listings

title: Understanding How eXp World Functions as a Virtual Office
excerpt: Explore eXp World 2.0, the virtual hub for real estate agents seeking to revolutionize their business with cutting-edge tools and global networking.
content:
Key Takeaway: eXp World is a browser-based virtual office that replaces physical broker offices by centralizing broker access, training, support, and collaboration in one persistent environment available to all eXp Realty agents worldwide.

TL;DR About eXp World at eXp Realty

  • eXp World is a virtual office environment, not a CRM or marketing tool
  • Provides live broker access, training, and support without physical offices
  • Available to all eXp Realty agents at no additional cost
  • Browser-based, no VR headset required
  • Optional to use, agents can access support through other channels
  • Designed to scale globally with real-time collaboration and translation

eXp World is eXp Realty’s browser-based virtual office environment designed to centralize broker access, training, support, and collaboration without reliance on physical office locations.

eXp Realty is always evolving, adding new features to keep you at the top of your game. Take eXp World, for instance – our revolutionary digital office space is now new and improved and accessible right from your web browser! And, yes, eXp Realty is the ONLY brokerage with a digital office metaverse.

Traditional brokerages rely on physical offices to deliver training, broker access, and community. eXp Realty replaced that infrastructure entirely with eXp World, a virtual office environment where agents can meet, train, and get real-time support without commuting or waiting for limited in-office availability.

This article explains how eXp World fits into the broader eXp Realty Technology ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

What’s eXp World?
Quick Start Guide to eXp World
Customize Your Avatar: Show Off Your Style in eXp World
Explore the Terrain: Live Tours
Customize Your Virtual Office for Clients
Ask a Broker, Get an Answer
Agent Support – Get Answers, Grab Opportunities
Transactions
Tech Support Outpost
Education Opportunities
Agent Solutions
What is Boldtrail?
Other eXp Realty Solutions
eXp Commercial
eXp World Entertainment Options
Community Vibes in eXp World
Mobile Accessibility
Future Innovations
What Agents Also Ask About eXp World
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Technology Guides

What’s eXp World?

Are you feeling somewhat lost because you don’t really understand what eXp World is? Well, you’re in for a treat! eXp World is a revolutionary digital cloud-based world crafted specifically for eXp real estate agents from around the globe.

And, when we say around the globe, we mean it. Supporting eXp’s presence in 27 global locations, the platform features real-time translation through closed captions. This feature ensures effortless communication across borders, allowing everyone to participate and understand discussions fully.

eXp World is engineered for collaboration, learning, and working together and with clients – all from the comfort of your couch. Yes, siree! eXp World leverages virtual reality to transform the real estate business, making your workday faster and smoother.

eXp’s cutting-edge technology supports seamless real-time collaboration, allowing you to share screens, hold meetings, and chat as naturally as if you were in the same room. Communication flows easily with instant messaging, voice chats, and video calls that feel just like face-to-face interactions.

eXp World Benefits

Imagine logging into a cloud-based customized office where you can meet your clients. Imagine getting all your problems solved easily and quickly. Imagine holding team meetings, joining training sessions, or brainstorming with colleagues. And, yes, you are talking to real people in eXp World, not bots!

Imagine easily getting all your issues solved like questions for brokers, transactions and tech issues, questions about eXp Realty programs and partners and so much more!

It’s all there in one location. No need to commute. No need to wait hours to get something resolved. It’s a game-changer for saving both time and money.

Side point. While you can do tons in eXp World – and eXp World is this blog’s topic – if it’s not your thing, no worries. Using eXp World is not mandatory. eXp offers several other connection methods too. For example, you can email your broker or call eXpert Care 24/7.

eXp Realty is about providing agent choices. That’s just one of the reasons eXp Realty is the largest independent real estate brokerage and the most agent-centric brokerage on the planet. Ok, let’s walk through eXp World together!

Quick Start Guide to eXp World

Navigating eXp World for the first time can feel like stepping into a new universe. Your journey begins with the website address, and clicking “connect. It’s super fast. Once inside eXp World, you’ll be in the “Welcome Area”. A vibrant, virtual landscape will greet you there.

But make no mistake! The first time you enter eXp World, you’ll be in the “Welcome Area”, but once in, you can bookmark any place to return directly there when you return. Ahhhh, the power of a web-based experience.

Back to your initial experience when you enter eXp World, it’s easy to check out the space, just click on the screen and move your mouse.

Getting around is also a breeze. Move forward and backward with your up and down keyboard arrow keys. Change directions with your left and right keyboard arrows. And, if you’d like to move faster, press your keyboard’s shift key to speed up.

Want to chat with someone? Just click the microphone icon to start talking.

Got a question? Ask with the Help button in the lower right, or chat with anyone through the chat button, or ask at the Welcome Desk. Get inside the blue box on the ground so the Welcome Desk staff can hear you.

Customize Your Avatar: Show Off Your Style in eXp World

When you first enter eXp World, you’ll be a robot. But don’t stay basic for long! Personalizing your avatar is fun and also reflects your unique identity or brand. Start your avatar customization by clicking the three bars at the top right. Choose “Profile”, then “Customize Avatar”.

Choose Avatar Type “Human” and then choose all the facets of your look from a variety of hairstyles, outfits, and accessories. Or choose “Android” and stay a robot but pick your color. Or choose “Ready Player Me” to use your picture to create the most life-like reflection of yourself.

Whether channeling your real-life style or creating a brand new persona, personalizing your avatar is all about self-expression. Plus, you can easily switch up your look for different meetings or just to keep things interesting.

Updating your avatar for special events or whenever inspiration hits is quick and enjoyable, ensuring you always make the perfect impression in every virtual interaction.

Explore the Terrain: Live Tours

Now that you’ve chosen your personal look, it’s time to get the lay of the land in eXp World. Head over to the Welcome Area desk and catch one of the live tours offered.

Currently, you can jump into one of those three times each weekday. To discover the times the tours are offered, click on the “eXp Events and Training Calendar”. It’s hard to miss!

Customize Your Virtual Office for Clients

In eXp World, customization extends beyond just avatars. You can style your own virtual office to meet with clients or colleagues! Get started by clicking the three bars at the top right and choosing “Frames”, then “Create Frame”.

Next, name your space and choose an environment from the myriad of choices. Then decorate your walls with images, PDFs, videos, whiteboards, polls, and streaming screens where you can share your webcam or your screen.

When it comes time for meetings, whether they’re one-on-one chats or large group brainstorming sessions, your Frame is a powerhouse. Screen sharing and virtual whiteboards make every meet-up more engaging, allowing you to share documents or hash out ideas on the fly.

You can also explore neighborhoods with clients using 360 photos and Google Street View right within the platform.

eXp World is built to handle any meeting type with ease. Its versatile setup ensures that you can tailor the experience for optimal productivity, no matter the size of your gathering.

Of course, remember that agents can also meet in person at one of the 3000 Regus office business center locations around the globe. So if the virtual office really doesn’t float your boat, eXp has your back. eXp agents can meet in person too at one of these business lounges – for free, it’s included in your monthly $85 fee.

Ask a Broker, Get an Answer

Broker questions are handled through live, state-specific rooms inside eXp World, replacing single-broker bottlenecks with routed, near-real-time support rather than scheduled office hours. eXp World is your go-to spot. Here, our brokers are ready to focus entirely on you. Furthermore, brokers are part of the eXp Realty staff. They are not paid from your commissions like at traditional brokerages. Every eXp agent gets the full, undivided attention of our brokers.

To get to your broker, just click “Go To,” then “US Broker Rooms,” select your state, and check-in. A friendly receptionist will guide you straight to the right expert. And guess what? You hardly ever have to wait! It sure beats tracking down the only broker available in a traditional brokerage office.

Pro tip: Bookmark your state’s broker room web URL for direct lightning-fast access to the broker’s office. Super handy!

Agent Support – Get Answers, Grab Opportunities

eXp World is your one-stop shop for all your queries and for discovering new opportunities. Let’s hit “Go To”, then “Agent Support” and explore!

Explore the Infosphere

Beam into the Infosphere at eXp World, where you’ll find a wealth of resources tailored for every aspect of your real estate career. Here you’ll find eXp Luxury, eXp Commercial, and eXp’s Referral Division. There’s also Onboarding, Mentor Program, ICON status, eXp University, eXp Revenos, State-specific resources, and even a video on how to fill out your join eXp Realty application.

Most of what is in Infosphere should be pretty self-explanatory. However, if you’re not on top of all that eXp Realty has to offer, you might not know about our awesome eXp Referrals and eXp Revenos divisions.

What is eXp Revenos?

Revenos is eXp’s engine to bring agents top-quality referrals. The division provides training and certification so you can be the agent awarded business that Revenos does all the work to get. For example, through Revenos you gain corporate transferee clients, bank-owned property clients, ibuyer clients, and more. Moreover, Revenos keeps identifying new referral opportunities for agents.

What is eXp Referrals?

Speaking of referrals, once you have one or give one to another agent, eXp has a referral tool to safeguard and document your referral agreement. In fact, eXp’s Referral tool makes sending and managing referrals straightforward. The tool automatically populates agent details, identifies necessary eSignatures, and ensures all paperwork is handled efficiently from start to finish. All you do is fill that form out and upload it into Skyslope. It’s super easy and quick.

Expert Care

Let’s get back to other eXp World areas for agent support. Take “Expert Care”, for example. Here’s your place to ask all your onboarding or general eXp Realty questions. Expert Care is open 24/7. So you’ll never need to wait to get your questions answered.

Transactions

Here’s another eXp Realty area that is open 24/7. Here you get all your transaction issues resolved. Staff members are ready to help agents with any issue, big or small.

Explore Accounting

And, then there’s “Accounting”. This is where you’ll head for questions or issues with payables, commissions, 1099’s, billing, revenue share, and stock questions. Hey, and if you don’t know what revenue share is, be sure to check out our blog or youtube on the topic. It’ provides a game-changing source of income.

Tech Support Outpost

Ok, and now we get to technical support, worthy of a bit more in-depth information. You see, eXp Realty is known throughout the industry as one of the most innovative technology brokerages. Our agents have access to some of the most powerful technologies in real estate. You’ll want to get to know our top technology tools that will help you successfully navigate eXp Realty and manage your business.

But, when you have a problem with our tech wonders, here’s where you go. Tech Support is open in eXp World from 8 am to 5 pm in all time zones. Go to tech support for your issues with Passport login, Workplace, and more. Don’t know what those are? Here are some short explanations:

What is eXp Passport?

eXp Passport is our single sign-on system. With your eXp Passport account, you can conveniently access many of the applications you typically use through a single, secure sign-on experience.

What is Workplace?

Workplace, made by Facebook, is an easy-to-use private collaboration platform for just eXp agents and staff. This is where we share best practices, keep up on company news and events, share referrals, and more. And, hey, it’s also another place to ask a question. Just type your question in on the search bar.

Education Opportunities

On to education opportunities in eXp World, there’s a lot! In fact, eXp World is a goldmine for continuous learning, offering over 50 hours of live training every week. These sessions are led by staff, brokers, and eXp’s top-performing ICON agents. The topics range widely, covering everything crucial to the industry.

Access the calendar in the Welcome Area, choose what interests you, and use the link to teleport to the correct room. Once inside, there’s always a method to zoom into the screen for easy viewing. Plus, all training is available on-demand, in case you can’t make a session that interests you.

Bottom line, whether you’re tuning in from across the globe or from the comfort of your home, eXp World brings top-notch training right to your screen.

Agent Solutions

Agent Solutions provides just what you’d expect – solutions to your agent needs. To get an overview “Go To” “Solutions”, then “eXp Solutions EXPO Hall”. Here you’ll see a complete suite of industry-leading vendors, tools, and services that agents can use throughout their real estate journey and can trust as an extension of their team. Let’s look at some of those:

What is Boldtrail?

BoldTrail is a #1 ranked real estate agent platform that all eXp Realty agents get as part of their $85 monthly fee. It’s a powerful CRM, that offers, lead generation, a personal IDX website, lead-to-contract management, listing promotion, and more. Agents outside of eXp Realty pay up to $500 a month to get Boldtrail.

What is Utility Connect?

Utility Connect is a free white glove concierge service for your clients who are moving. This will simplify their move and save time and money using Utility Connect’s integrated platform to compare utility providers, get special offers, and connect your client’s essential home services all in one place.

What is eXp Realty Healthcare?

eXp Reality offers a healthcare option where agents save money and reduce risk. Clearwater Benefits offers several healthcare plan options to meet all agent’s unique needs. Agents save an average of $10,000 a year on fixed medical costs and 80% on out-of-pocket costs with Clearwater. Plus, agents have access to one of the largest PPO networks in the country.

What is eXp’s Making It Rain?

eXp’s Making It Rain is a lead generation program for agents lacking time or expertise to leverage digital ad power. Making it Rain automates digital marketing programs for agents with products built to drive more ad results at a lower cost. Sophisticated advertising made simple!

What is Curbio?

Curbio is eXp Realty’s solution for fast and reliable pre-listing home updates – like interior renovations, exterior improvements, and staging. No project is too big or too small. eXp agents get free, same-day estimates. Best for agents, you hand over the project to be fully managed by Curbio. Best for your client – they get competitive pricing, sell their home faster and for more, and pay nothing until their home sale closes.

What is eCommission?

eCommission is eXp Realty’s solution to solve agent cash flow gaps between deals by giving you access to your real estate commissions before closing. From top producers to agents just starting out, cash flow ups and downs are common to almost everyone selling real estate and eCommission has got your back.

Other eXp Realty Solutions

Other eXp Realty agent solutions that you’ll find in eXp World include Success Lending for great lending rates, ExpressOffers & Opendoor for cash offers, Zenlist – to view eXp Exclusives (off-market properties), Property Management – for your clients that need it, and Blended Sense, a media technology platform making top quality listing productions accessible and reliable for agents and many more.

eXp Commercial

Another place to visit in eXp World is eXp Commercial. While there’s a commercial booth in the Infosphere, eXp Commercial’s significance earns it a dedicated space too. Just hit “Go To” and select “Commercial” to teleport straight there.

Whether you’re looking to expand your knowledge or explore new opportunities in commercial real estate, this specialized area has everything you need to get started and thrive in the bustling commercial sector.

eXp World Entertainment Options

In eXp World, you can flex your creative muscles by crafting anything you envision within a Frame. You’ll set up your entertainment space using the same simple process we described for creating your office space.

These versatile environments offer a unique way to host client appreciation events. Picture guiding your clients through a virtual art gallery or enjoying a live concert together online. It’s a fresh and engaging approach to bring people together, connect with clients, and enhance your professional relationships.

Community Vibes in eXp World

Being a real estate agent can sometimes feel isolating. Enter eXp World – no need to get dressed or get in your car! Just hop online and chat with other agents who are just a click away. Just open your mic and start gabbing.

eXp World is buzzing with agents making connections. It’s a real social mixer without the awkward handshakes. Many eXp agents report that a quick conversation in eXp World led to a great partnership or a successful deal. This virtual world isn’t just about the chats; it’s a goldmine for networking and building real, profitable connections.

Mobile Accessibility

Are you wondering if you can access eXp World’s all-encompassing platform from your phone? No sweat! eXp World’s got you covered with its mobile-friendly setup.

Dive into the action from your smartphone or tablet and snag almost all the features you’d find on the desktop version. Sure, a few bells and whistles might be tweaked to fit your screen, but it’s all about keeping you in the loop seamlessly—whether you’re couch surfing or sealing deals in the streets.

Plus, eXp’s developers are on their toes, pushing updates that dial-up its usability and ensure you’re never left hanging. It’s all about making your mobile real estate hustle as smooth as possible, wherever you might be!

Future Innovations

eXp Realty is always on top of providing the best of the best. Plus, your thoughts and suggestions are super important. eXp World thrives on user feedback, transforming your bright ideas into real-world improvements. So don’t hesitate to speak up—your input might just spark the next big update!

And let’s not forget, eXp Realty isn’t just keeping pace—they’re setting the pace, leading the charge with innovations that leave agents in awe. With a commitment to always push boundaries, eXp World is on track to keep its spot as the premier virtual platform for real estate pros. From smoother navigation to more immersive environments, there’s always something new in the pipeline. Stay tuned, because eXp is all about giving you the cutting-edge tools you need to succeed.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp World

Is eXp World required to be used by all eXp Realty agents?

No. eXp World is optional. Agents may choose to use it daily, occasionally, or not at all. Broker support, training, and assistance are also available through phone, email, and Expert Care, allowing agents to select the access method that fits their workflow.

How does eXp World replace a physical real estate office?

eXp World replaces physical offices by centralizing broker access, training, meetings, and support in a virtual environment. Instead of traveling to an office, agents enter live rooms for brokers, classes, and collaboration, reducing overhead while increasing availability and consistency.

Who benefits most from using eXp World?

Agents who value fast broker access, ongoing education, and collaboration benefit most. New agents often use it for training and support, while experienced agents use it for broker questions, meetings, and networking without geographic limitations.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty technology is designed to address agent use cases like broker access, training, and collaboration, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do all eXp Realty agents have access to eXp World?

Yes. All eXp Realty agents receive access to eXp World as part of the standard monthly technology fee. There are no usage limits, attendance requirements, or additional costs tied to accessing broker rooms, training, or support areas.

Does eXp World require virtual reality equipment?

No. eXp World is browser-based and does not require VR headsets or specialized hardware. Agents access the platform using a standard computer browser, making it accessible without additional technology purchases.

Can agents meet clients inside eXp World?

Yes. Agents can host client meetings in private virtual offices using screen sharing, media tools, and interactive features. While optional, this provides an alternative to in-person or video meetings, particularly for remote or relocation clients.

Is eXp World available on mobile devices?

Yes. eXp World offers mobile access with most core functionality available on smartphones and tablets. Some features are optimized for desktop use, but agents can access meetings, events, and support while on the go.

Does eXp World replace CRM or transaction management systems?

No. eXp World is an access and collaboration environment. CRM systems, transaction management, and compliance tools operate separately within eXp Realty’s technology platform. Each system serves a distinct role.

  • Homes for Sale: Way More Than Just Another Brokerage Website
  • Referral Agent: Your Guide to Easy Referring Success
